title: Pod Restart Policies
description:
## Pod Restart Policies

A pod runs a one-time database migration script. If the script **fails** (non-zero exit), the container should be restarted. If it **succeeds** (exit 0), it should **not** be restarted.

Which `restartPolicy` should be set in the pod spec?

options.text: `Always` — restarts the container regardless of exit code (this is the default)
`OnFailure` — restarts only if the container exits with a non-zero code
`Never` — the container is never restarted, even on failure
`OnCompletion` — restarts the container only after it completes successfully

explanation: `OnFailure` is the right policy for batch workloads like migration scripts — Kubernetes restarts the container on any non-zero exit code but leaves it alone after a clean exit (code 0). `Always` (the default for Deployment pods) would keep restarting even after success, creating an infinite restart loop. `Never` means no automatic recovery on failure. `OnCompletion` does not exist in Kubernetes — the three valid values are `Always`, `OnFailure`, and `Never`. Jobs automatically use `OnFailure` by default.

hints.title: restartPolicy values
hints.body: Three valid values: `Always` (default for Pods), `OnFailure` (for Jobs/batch), `Never` (run-once, no retry). Check with `kubectl explain pod.spec.restartPolicy`.
